title: South African Renewable Energy Masterplan
description: This plan has a vision to 2030 and aims to develop renewable energy in the country. Key objectives are to foster renewables energy and battery storage project, to expand the industrial capacity in the renewable energy and storage value chain to create and sustain decent employment across the value chain, to shift to a more low-carbon electricity supply industry.

title: Law 109/2025 on greenhouse gas emissions tax
description: Serbia introduced taxes on greenhouse gas emissions (CO2, N2O and PFCs) starting from January 2026. The tax cover the production of electricity, cement, aluminium, artificial fertilizers and nitrogen compounds and crude iron, steel, and ferroalloys. The tax rate is set at four euros per ton of CO2 equivalent.Â

title: Carbon Fee System under Climate Change Response Act
description:
The Climate Change Response Act establishes a carbon fee system in Taiwan, targeting large emitters including power generation and industrial sectors. The system applies to facilities emitting more than 25,000 tonnes of COâ per year and is designed to incentivise emissions reductions through economic signals.

The carbon fee was implemented from 2025, with payments starting in 2026, and an initial rate of approximately NT$300 per tonne of COâ. Covered entities are required to report emissions and may submit reduction plans to qualify for preferential rates.

title: Development of village electricity networks and community gas networks
description: The budget for the Ministry of Energy and Mineral Resources has been set at USD 1.3 million (21.67 billion Indonesian rupiah) for the 2026 financial year; it will be used for the development of the town gas network, oil, gas and coal exploration activities, and the programme aimed at increasing the electrification rate.

title: Value-Added Tax and Luxury Goods Tax for certain electric vehicles
description: The regulation reduces or exempts taxes on electric cars and buses to incentivise electric vehicle adoption in Indonesia, support low-emission transport transition and reduce upfront cost of EVs for consumers

title: Citizens Energy Package
description: The Citizens Energy Package aims to tackle energy poverty by supporting Member States in reducing electricity taxes and levies for households, fostering the development of energy communities, encouraging the uptake of flexible retail electricity contracts, introducing enhanced safeguards against disconnections and long-term structural reforms.

title: Innovation Fund - Battery Booster Facility
description: The Commission adopted the Communication on a Battery Booster Strategy, establishing the Battery Booster Facility. The Facility aims to mobilise up to â¬1.5 billion from the Innovation Fund to support European battery cell producers during the ramp-up phase of their projects.Â

title: Clean Energy Investment Strategy
description: The Strategy aims to mobilise investment in the energy sector by improving the link between private capital and Europeâs pipeline of energy projects. To support this strategy, the European Investment Bank intends to deliver more than â¬75 billion of financing over 3 years.

title: Incentives for energy renovation to combat energy poverty
description: Ministry of Environmental Protection and Green Transition has launched a USD 708 million (EUR 652 million) package for 2025 to offers subsidies for energy efficiency measures in family homes, targeting households at risk of energy poverty amid rising energy bills.

title: Programme for the Procurement of Vehicles with Alternative Drivetrains for Public Local and Regional Transport
description: The programme is a funding initiative for the procurement of battery-electric buses and associated charging infrastructure with a mobilised budget of USD 177 million (EUR 163 million) to support the acquisition of 206 new electric buses.

title: Programme to support strategic investments 2025-2033
description: The programme aims at attracting investments for the domestic manufacturing of clean energy technologies, notably batteries, solar panels, wind turbines, heat pumps and electrolysers.Â

title: Clean Industrial Deal State Aid Framework
description: Within the framework of the EU Clean Industrial Deal State Aid Framework, Greece set a EUR 400 million scheme to support clean technology manufacturing capacity, along with renewable fuels, and measures allowing temporary electricity price relief for energy-intensive industries.

title: AAP DECARB FLASH
description:
AAP DECARB FLASH 2025â2027 aims to fund initiatives focusing on:

  • the recovery of waste heat;
  • energy efficiency;
  • changes to the energy mix;
  • building insulation;
  • reducing fossil fuel consumption through the generation of renewable energy.Â

title: Major Industrial Decarbonisation Projects
description: This investment programme will support electrification, improving energy efficiency, changing the energy mix or the materials mix, CCUS projects. 4 plants (1 aluminium and 3 cement) are targeted together with CO2 transport infrastructure.

title: Scheme for manufacturing capacity for clean technologies
description: The Hungarian government designed a USD 4.5 billion (EUR 4.1 billion) aid scheme to expand manufacturing capacity for clean technologies, such as batteries and solar panels, in line with the EU's net-zero transition goals. The initiative provides grants and tax advantages until 2030 to incentivise strategic investments in the production of net-zero components across the entire country.Â

title: Home Energy Storage Programme
description: The programme provides a USD 6,800 (HUF 2.5 million ) non-refundable subsidy for families to install energy storage systems with a minimum 10 kWh capacity, aiming to increase self-sufficiency and lower electricity bills.Â

title: Malta updated National Energy and Climate Plan
description:
Malta updated its National Energy and Climate Plan with new energy-related targets and policies: Â 
  • 25% renewable energy share by 2030 through expanded solar PV and new offshore wind policies
  • Commissioning a second interconnector with Italy and installing large-scale battery storage systems
  • Renovation of public buildings to reduce primary demand and replacing 34,000 streetlights with LEDs

title: Clean Transition Aid Scheme for Industrial Investments
description: Finland introduced a â¬400 million aid scheme in 2025 to support industrial investments that decarbonise production processes and improve energy efficiency. The programme provides government grants for projects with eligible costs of at least â¬30 million, targeting emissions reductions, electrification, and efficiency improvements in key industrial sectors.Â

title: Climate Action Plan 2025 (CAP25)
description:
CAP25 is the latest annual update to Irelandâs national climate strategy, prepared under the Climate Action and Low Carbon Development (Amendment) Act 2021. It provides a vertically integrated policy framework that aligns national planning with legally binding carbon budgets and sectoral emissions ceilings. The plan is designed to be read in conjunction with CAP24, focusing on "turbocharging delivery" and completing high-impact legacy actions.

Core Energy and Climate Targets (by 2030):
  • Renewable Electricity: Increase the share of renewable electricity generation to 80%.
  • Capacity Targets: Achievment of 9 GW of onshore wind, 8 GW of solar, and at least 5 GW of offshore wind.
  • Buildings: Retrofit 500,000 homes and install 400,000 heat pumps in existing buildings.
  • Energy Efficiency: A 50% improvement in public sector energy efficiency.
  • Transport: A 20% reduction in total vehicle kilometres travelled and a 50% reduction in fuel usage.

Governance and Funding:
  • Oversight: The Department of the Taoiseach oversees quarterly progress reporting to ensure cross-departmental coordination.
  • Investment: Budget 2025 allocated â¬951 million in carbon tax revenues to fund these initiatives.
  • Strategic Alignment: The plan integrates with the National Energy and Climate Plan (NECP) and the Long-term Climate Strategy, providing the link between 2030 targets and mid-century neutrality.

title: Business Energy Upgrades Scheme (2025)
description: Â Launched in April 2025, the Business Energy Upgrades Scheme provides capital grants to SMEs and enterprises for energy efficiency and decarbonisation investments. Grants support measures such as heat pumps (up to ~â¬100,000), solar PV, heat recovery systems, and building fabric upgrades, with pre-approved fixed payments and rapid approval processes. The scheme also funds energy audits and advisory services to design projects. It targets reductions in energy costs and emissions across commercial and industrial buildings, complementing broader SEAI programmes that have delivered over â¬175 million in funding to 13,000 businesses in recent years.Â

title: Strategic Gas Emergency Reserve (FSRU)
description: In March 2025, Ireland approved a state-led strategic gas emergency reserve to strengthen energy security and ensure compliance with the EU N-1 infrastructure standard. The reserve will be delivered as a Floating Storage and Regasification Unit (FSRU), owned and operated by Gas Networks Ireland on behalf of the State. It is designed as a non-commercial, emergency-only facility, activated during supply disruptions, and explicitly defined as a temporary transitional measure to avoid fossil fuel lock-in while Ireland expands renewable energy and reduces import dependence.Â

title: Electricity storage facilities support programme
description: Lithuania implemented a programme to support the deployment of electricity storage facilities through multiple funding calls in 2025. The scheme includes EUR 45 million allocated in October 2025, EUR 37.3 million in additional funding following oversubscription, and EUR 18 million in earlier grants, with subsidies covering up to 30% of project costs. Eligible projects include high-capacity storage installations (typically â¥15 MW). Applications exceeded EUR 197 million, with planned deployment exceeding 1 700 MW and 4 000 MWh of storage capacity, aiming to enhance system flexibility and support renewable energy integration.

title: Schemes to support renewable energy projects by households
description: The scheme is a USD 11.2 million (EUR 10.3 million) investment designed to support households in installing photovoltaic systems and battery storage.Â

title: Incentive System for Climate and Energy Transition (SITCE)
description: SITCE is a Portuguese incentive system under Compete 2030 focused on supporting company decarbonisation and energy efficiency. It funds the transition to low-carbon technologies, equipment adaptation, and the integration of renewable energy sources. With a USD 179 million (EUR 165 million) FEDER budget, the programme provides co-financing rates of up to 85% for eligible companies of various sizes.Â

title: Grants for the purchase of electric taxis
description: The initiative is a USD 1.08 million (EUR 1 million) tender to the national taxi sector. It provides grants of up to â¬6,000 for the purchase of electric vehicles and an additional USD 6,500 (EUR 6,000) for scrapping taxis older than ten years.Â

title: Investment to the installation of battery energy storage systems
description: This funding of USD 65.5 million (EUR 60.25 million) from the Recovery and Resilience Plan (PRR) is specifically directed toward private renewable energy producers for the installation of battery energy storage systems connected to the public network.

title: Scheme to Promote Manufacturing of Sintered Rare Earth Permanent Magnet
description:
The Scheme aims to establish fully integrated domestic manufacturing ecosystem for rare-earth permanent magnets (REPMs) in India, from oxide feedstocks to end products. It has a budgetary outlay of INR 7 280 crore (~ 670 MEUR) and intends to create manufacturing capacity of 6 000 metric tonnes per annum (MTPA) of sintered REPMs.

The key features of the Scheme are listed below:
  1. Beneficiaries: The manufacturing capacity of 6 000 MTPA will be allocated among 5 beneficiaries through bidding;
  2. Incentives: The beneficiaries will receive sales-linked incentive of INR 6,450 crore (~ 590 MEUR) on the sale of sintered REPMs over 5 years (after a 2-year gestation period);
  3. Capital Subsidy: The beneficiaries will receive capital subsidies of INR 750 crore (~ 70 MEUR) for setting up their manufacturing capacity;
  4. Feedstock: Three lowest-bidding beneficiaries will receive an assured limited supply of rare-earth oxides from IREL (India) Ltd, a public sector undertaking.

title: Clean technology manufacturing support scheme 2026-2030
description: Under the EU Clean Industry Pact framework, Luxembourg set up a direct subsidy scheme amounting EUR 500 million aimed at supporting domestic clean technology manufacturing. These include solar and wind technologies, heat pumps, and battery technologies.

title: Lower Fuel Prices Package
description:
The Lower Fuel Prices (CPN) package includes reducing fuel VAT and excise taxes, and introducing a maximum price at the pump
Â - Poland has introduced maximum retail prices for petrol and diesel. Retailers caught selling above the cap face fines of up to 1 million zloty (â¬233,000)
Â - Poland has announced temporary reductions in both VAT and excise duty on fuel (petrol, diesel and biocomponents constituting self-contained fuels). The package includes VAT reduction on fuels (from 23% to 8%) and excise duty reduction (lowered to the minimum allowed under EU rules). VAT reduction are foreseen to end on 31.05.2026, and 31.05.2026 for the excise duty reduction.Â

title: National Programme on Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) Battery Storage
description: The National Programme on Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) Battery Storage has a total outlay of â¹18,100 Crore for a capacity of 50 GWh for a period of 5 years after gestation period of 2 years.

title: Assistance in Deploying Energy Efficient Technologies in Industries & Establishments (ADEETIE)
description: ADEETIE (Assistance in Deploying Energy Efficient Technologies in Industries & Establishments) is a â¹1000 crore Indian government scheme to help Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises adopt energy-efficient technologies. Managed by the Bureau of Energy Efficiency, it provides 3-5% interest subvention on loans alongside technical support like energy audits and detailed project reports.Â

title: Key Research Directions for Green and Low-Carbon Standards in Industry and Information Technology (2025-2027)
description: The Ministry of Industry and Information Technology's plan highlights its key reseach focus for 2025-2027, around advanced manufacturing, carbon management, energy efficiency, waste heat recovery, hydrogen, data centre, recycling, electric vehicles, battery and water saving in industry..

title: Investment in battery innovation and production projects
description: Natural Resources Canada invested over USD 16 million (CAD 22 million) in support of eight projects to accelerate battery innovation and production capacity. More specifically, the investment will support key priorities for Canada, namely enhancing battery performance and reducing costs, strengthening energy security and improving supply chain competitiveness, and supporting the development and scale-up of new technologies.

title: Electric Vehicle Affordability Program (EVAP)
description: Canada launched a new EV affordability incentive programme, offering up to USD 3 650 (CAD 5 000) for batteryâelectric and fuelâcell vehicles and USD 1800 (CAD 2 500) for plugâin hybrids, for vehicles purchased or leased. The programme targets lowerâcost EVs, supports domestic manufacturing and complements investments in charging infrastructure. To qualify, EVs must be made in Canada or in countries that have free-trade agreements with Canada.

description: The programme aims at promoting projects for the conversion and efficiency of fuel consumption and for energy efficiency in electricity use in the industrial sector.Â

title: Transition from Rapid Expansion to System Integration
description: The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy released its new priorities, highlighting Indiaâs transition from rapid expansion to system integration, ensuring stable, reliable, and sustainable renewable growth. The strategy prioritizes "firm and dispatchable" power through hybrid projects, RTC supply, and Battery Energy Storage Systems.Â

title: Renewable Energy Policy 2025
description: The policy sets targets to meet 20% of the nationâs power demand through renewable sources by 2030 and 30% by 2040. It provides financial incentives, including a 10-year corporate income tax exemption and duty-free imports for renewable energy components and raw materials. The framework introduces regulatory mandates such as Renewable Purchase Obligations (RPO) and a certificate mechanism to encourage utility-scale and private sector investment. Its scope covers diverse technologies including solar, wind, green hydrogen, and waste-to-energy, while emphasising grid stability through Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS)

title: Customs duty exemptions on import of critical minerals scrap and capital goods for manufacturing EV batteries
description:
In its national budget for financial year (FY) 2025-2026, India fully exempted the basic customs duty on the import of Cobalt powder and waste and scrap of 12 critical minerals listed below:Â 
  • Antimony
  • Beryllium
  • Bismuth
  • Cobalt
  • Cadmium
  • Molybdenum
  • Rhenium
  • Tantalum
  • Tin
  • Tungsten
  • Zirconium
  • Copper scrap (limited tariff entries)
In addition, the budget also exempted the basic customs duty on the import of waste and scrap of lithium-ion battery and on capital goods for EV battery manufacturing.

title: Customs duty exemptions on import of goods for processing critical minerals
description:
In its national budget for financial year (FY) 2026-2027, India announced an exemption from the basic customs duty levied on import of:
  • capital goods required for processing of critical minerals in India
  • capital goods used for manufacturing lithium-ion cells for battery energy storage systems.
These exemptions have been made with a view to support domestic processing of critical minerals and strengthen infrastructure required for India's energy transition.

title: Mines and Minerals (Development and Regulation) Amendment Act, 2025
description:
The Mines and Minerals (Development and Regulation) Amendment Act, 2025 amends the Mines and Minerals (Development and Regulation) Act, 1957, building on earlier reforms, particularly the 2023 amendment, which introduced an exploration licensing regime and a framework for the auction and development of critical and deepâseated minerals. The 2025 amendment aims to accelerate mineral production, enhance operational flexibility and promote the development of a minerals market in India.

The key policy actions taken under this amendment are set out below:
  • Expanded exploration and development financing: The amendment expands the scope of the National Mineral Exploration and Development Trust, allowing its funds to be used for onshore, offshore and overseas exploration and development. The contribution amounts payable by lessees into the Trust have also been increased from 2% to 3% of royalty.
  • Inclusion of additional minerals: The amendment allows for the inclusion of new minerals in existing mining leases, with no additional amounts payable for critical or strategic seated minerals.
  • Extension of mining areas: The amendment allows for a oneâtime extension of up to 10% (mining leases) or 30% (composite licences) of contiguous lease area to promote the mining of deepâseated minerals.
  • Development of minerals market: The amendment empowers the Central Government to establish mineral exchanges for transparent trading of minerals, its concentrate or its processed forms, with a view to promote fair and transparent price discovery.
  • Liberalised sale from captive mines: The amendment removes caps on sale of surplus production from captive mines after meeting endâuse requirements and permits sale of legacy mineral dumps which cannot be captively utilised.
  • Faster mineral auctions: Removes the requirement for prior Central Government approval for auctions of bauxite, iron ore, limestone and manganese under composite licences to expedite auctions.

title: Sustainable Jobs Action Plan 2026-2030
description:
The 2026-2030 Sustainable Jobs Action Plan was published as required by the Sustainabe Jobs Act.

The Action Plan is centered around three pillars:
  • Build Canada Strong
  • Empower workers
  • Protect industries

The Action Plan outlines several new and existing mechanisms to achieve its goals:
  • Major Projects Office: Established to streamline regulatory approvals for large-scale nation-building projects.
  • Clean Economy Investment Tax Credits: Incentives to attract investment in technologies like carbon capture, clean hydrogen, and clean technology manufacturing.
  • Workforce Alliances: The government invests USD 279.5 million (CAN 382.9 million) over five years, starting in 2026-27, and USD 41 million (CAN 56.1 million) ongoing, to launch new bodies that bring together employers, unions, and training institutions to coordinate workforce development strategies in priority areas like housing, energy, and mining.
  • Strategic Response Fund: A USD 3.6 billion (CAN 5 billion) fund aimed at supporting transformative projects in industries impacted by trade disputes, such as steel, aluminum, and automotive sectors

title: Climate Competitiveness Strategy
description: Released in Budget 2025, Canada's Climate Competitiveness Strategy positions climate action as a lever for "boosting competitiveness and building a strong economy". The strategy advances aims to increase investment certainty, and commits to strengthening industrial carbon pricing and developing a post-2030 price trajectory. The strategy also announces plans to introduce legislation to deliver a clean electricity investment tax credit and enhance existing clean technology tax credits by expanding eligibility.

description: Renewable Energy Expansion Act (EAG) subsidies are investment grants for photovoltaic systems and battery storage as well as support for other technologies, including hydropower, wind energy, biomass and biogas.
